pi_items.source: Task description (line 6): 'Service connection name: LegacyInventorySystem'. Matches task.json metadata and verifier.py implementation.

Task description (line 2): 'Create a new Generic service connection'. Confirmed as valid Azure DevOps service connection type per Microsoft Learn documentation (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/pipelines/library/service-endpoints). Matches verifier.py which checks for generic type.
Task description (line 3): 'Server URL: https://inventory.local/api/v1'. This is a synthetic/test URL, not a real public service.
Task description (line 4): 'Username: svc_ado_connect'. This is a synthetic/test credential.
Task description (line 1) and setup_azure_devops.ps1 (line 138) which creates project with this name. Confirmed as the project where the service connection will be created.
setup_azure_devops.ps1 (line 24) and task_utils.ps1 (line 17) both reference this URL as a candidate Azure DevOps Server collection URL.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a generic service connection named 'LegacyInventorySystem' in the TailwindTraders project on a locally-hosted Azure DevOps Server at http://localhost/DefaultCollection. The connection connects to a synthetic test endpoint (https://inventory.local/api/v1) with synthetic credentials (username: svc_ado_connect). The verifier validates five aspects: existence of the connection, correct type (generic), matching URL and username, and pipeline access authorization. All metadata values are correctly specified and consistent across task.json, task description, and verification logic.
